questions.original_question: 2024年9月27日签署的股权转让协议完成之后，LTIC的股权结构是怎样的
questions.answer:
在2024年9月27日签署的股权转让协议完成后，路特斯科技创新中心有限公司（LTIC）的股权结构如下：吉利英国有限公司（Geely UK Limited）持有LTIC 80%的股权，而路特斯科技创新有限公司（LTIL）则持有剩余的20%股权。这一股权结构反映了双方在协议中的具体安排。

以上信息基于2024年11月21日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

rag_info.chunk_content: The amount payable for this transaction has been settled in September 2022. On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC), through its subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at the consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) purchased a one-year convertible note (the “Note”) with the principal of US$10.0 million issued by Ecarx. Upon the listing of Ecarx on Nasdaq on December 21, 2022, the Note was automatically converted into the Class A ordinary shares of Ecarx (the “listed equity securities”) at a conversion price of US$9.5 per share. As of June 30, 2023 and December 31, 2022, the fair value of the listed equity securities was US$7.3 million and US$8.4 million, respectively. In September 2021, Lotus Technology's wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Ningbo Juhe Yinqing Enterprise Management Consulting Partnership (Limited Partnership) provided guarantees and other features to the investor, which were accounted for as shareholder contributions at their estimated fair value at the respective issuance date of each tranche of loans.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into a Put Option Agreement with Geely and Etika in conjunction with the Original Merger Agreement. This agreement allows Geely and Etika to require LTC to repurchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The repurchase price will be determined based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year 2024, adjusted for net debt. The put options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, contingent upon Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Other Related Party Transactions 
summary: Lotus Tech has been actively involved in the sale of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), auto parts, and providing research and development (R&D) and consulting services, particularly to related parties. In the first half of 2023, the company reported a significant increase in service revenue, reaching $5.0 million, up from $1.9 million in the same period of 2022. Product sales also saw growth, with $807,000 in BEVs and auto parts sold to related parties in early 2023, compared to no sales in the previous year. By mid-2023, accounts receivable from related parties stood at $13.6 million, reflecting a consistent upward trend from previous years.

Lotus Tech's financial activities also include substantial transactions related to licensing agreements, notably with Zhejiang Liankong, a subsidiary of Geely Holding. Initially, the licensing cost was set at approximately $888.2 million but was later reduced to around $306.3 million. This license, which is limited to specific R&D projects, has been recorded as an expense, indicating no alternative future use. Additionally, the company has engaged in various financial arrangements with related parties, including R&D service payments, salary and expense reimbursements, and unsecured loans, which have contributed to a growing receivable balance.

The company has also entered into significant lease agreements for office and parking spaces, resulting in the recognition of right-of-use assets. In terms of purchases from related parties, Lotus Tech reported a dramatic increase to $264.4 million in the first half of 2023, compared to $10.1 million in the same period of 2022. This surge included substantial costs of goods sold and inventory levels.

Lotus Tech's financial obligations to related parties have increased over time, with amounts owed for various services and products rising significantly. The company has also engaged in equity transactions, including the acquisition of subsidiaries and investments in convertible notes, further expanding its financial landscape. Recent agreements, such as the distribution rights with Lotus Cars Limited and the trademark license agreements, highlight Lotus Tech's strategic partnerships and growth initiatives.

Overall, Lotus Tech's financial activities reflect a robust engagement with related parties, characterized by increasing revenues, significant investments in R&D, and a growing portfolio of agreements that position the company for future growth in the electric vehicle market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into put option agreements with each of Geely HK and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL), during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) as of December 31, 2024, at a future date and upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions. During March to April 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into convertible note purchase agreements with certain investors. One of the investors of Pre-IPO Notes, a related party controlled by the controlling shareholder of Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC), agreed to subscribe to a convertible note for a cash consideration of US$9 million, which has not been issued as of September 30, 2023. On November 6, 2023, the subscription amount by the related party controlled by the controlling shareholder of Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) changed from US$9 million to US$9.5 million, which has not been issued as of the issuance of the unaudited condensed financial statements.

summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into put option agreements with Geely and Etika as part of the Original Merger Agreement. This arrangement is designed to ensure that both Geely and Etika have a clear exit strategy while aligning their interests with LTC's performance in the upcoming fiscal year.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Following the initial kick-off meeting and consistent with the proposed terms in the Letter of Intent (LOI) that the global distribution functions of LGIL would be combined with Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) in connection with the Proposed Business Combination, beginning from the second half of November 2022, representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp (LCAA) and Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) and their respective advisors also engaged in multiple discussions and exchanged correspondences about the proposed terms of the Distribution Agreement pursuant to which Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC, would be appointed as the global distributor for Lotus Cars Limited (the entity carrying out Lotus Technology's sportscar manufacturing operations and an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of LGIL) for vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and, in connection with its role as global distributor, would provide after-sale services for the vehicles, parts, and tools distributed, with the most significant exchanges summarized in further detail below. On November 17, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) began providing confidential information to representatives and advisors of L Catterton Acquisition Corp (LCAA) regarding LTC’s business and operations through a virtual data room. During the period from November 17, 2022 to the parties’ entry into the Original Merger Agreement on January 31, 2023, LCAA and its representatives and advisors conducted extensive due diligence on LTC.

title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA are the result of extensive negotiations following LCAA's establishment as a blank check company in January 2021 by affiliates of L Catterton. LCAA's primary goal is to facilitate business combinations, and it raised $294.7 million through its IPO in March 2021. Although LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, including seven specific companies, it ultimately focused on LTC for a proposed merger. 

The discussions between LCAA and LTC began in early 2022, with LCAA representatives conducting thorough due diligence on LTC's business and financial performance. Throughout the negotiation process, valuation discussions fluctuated due to market conditions, with LTC's enterprise value initially proposed at $12 billion but later adjusted to a range of $5.5 billion to $10 billion. 

On January 31, 2023, the parties finalized key agreements, including the Original Merger Agreement, which established the terms for the business combination. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made, including provisions for PIPE financing and adjustments to the Sponsor Support Agreement. The merger aims to enhance strategic growth and operational efficiencies for both entities, with a focus on leveraging synergies to create shareholder value.

As of October 2023, the parties signed an amended agreement that revised the structure of the merger, ensuring that LCAA Public Shareholders would receive LTC American Depositary Shares instead of Ordinary Shares. Additionally, the Sponsor Support Agreement was amended to adjust the terms regarding the forfeiture and vesting of Sponsor Shares, reflecting the evolving nature of the partnership. Overall, the merger is positioned to capitalize on market opportunities and strengthen the combined entity's competitive positioning.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Liquidity and Capital Resources 
summary: The summary of cash flows and working capital provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance across various periods, emphasizing cash inflows and outflows, liquidity trends, and operational efficiency. The data covers two six-month periods ending June 30 for 2023 and 2022, as well as full years ending December 31 for 2022 and 2021, detailing key metrics in operating, investing, and financing activities, along with the effects of exchange rate changes.

Key findings indicate a notable increase in net cash used in operating activities, with losses escalating significantly in both the six-month and full-year comparisons. In contrast, cash outflows from investing activities showed a decrease in the latest six-month period, while the previous full year experienced a shift from positive inflows to negative outflows. Financing activities, however, provided substantial cash inflows, reflecting a strong reliance on financing to support operations.

The impact of exchange rate fluctuations was detrimental, contributing to losses in cash balances. The company's cash position revealed a decline in net cash and restricted cash in 2023 compared to the previous year, despite an increase in opening cash balances. The company has historically funded its operations through various financing means, including convertible notes and capital contributions, but now faces challenges in securing additional liquidity for ongoing operations.

To address these challenges, the company is exploring multiple strategies for securing funding, including potential external financing linked to a Business Combination and issuing new convertible notes. However, there is uncertainty regarding the success of these strategies and the company's ability to continue operations amid ongoing profitability issues and negative cash flows.

Additionally, the company has entered into various agreements related to convertible notes and exchangeable notes, which may impact future financing needs and operational strategies. The company generates revenue in RMB and anticipates ongoing challenges related to foreign currency conversion and regulatory approvals, which could further complicate its financial stability.

In summary, while the company has a strong cash position relative to previous years, it faces significant cash flow challenges, increased operating losses, and reliance on financing, necessitating strategic improvements and liquidity management to ensure future operational viability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 27, 2023, following discussions between Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) with Geely and Etika, respectively, regarding the proposed call option arrangement for the Capitalization Plan, representatives of LTC proposed to representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp (LCAA) that, rather than granting LTC a call option, each of Geely and Etika be granted a put option to sell their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technology Sdn Bhd (or such other entity holding the equity interests in Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) at the time when the call options are exercised) to LTC. The exercise of the put option would be subject to the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by the LGIL group in 2024 exceeds 5,000. Following additional communications that same day, LCAA agreed to the change from the call option arrangement to the put option arrangement. On the same day, representatives of Skadden circulated drafts of the Put Option Agreements to representatives of LCAA and Kirkland & Ellis (K&E), reflecting the foregoing change in option arrangement. Subsequently and up until the execution of the Put Option Agreements on January 31, 2023, some final comments were exchanged between representatives of Skadden and K&E. Also on January 27, 2023, Mr. Feng and Mr. Lee from Lotus Technology Corporation and Mr. Bhagat and Mr. Jin from L Catterton Acquisition Corp discussed the next steps for the Proposed Business Combination and set January 31, 2023 as the target signing date.

title: LTIC Share Transfer Agreement 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the LTIC Share Transfer Agreement**

Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., has executed a Share Transfer Agreement with Geely UK Limited (Geely) for the sale of 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (LTIC), a German company that is also a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL. This agreement marks a significant strategic move for both LTIL and Geely, reflecting Geely's increased stake in LTIC and reinforcing its position in the market.

title: Forward-Looking Statements 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Financial Performance for Lotus Technology Inc. (as of September 30, 2024)

This press release includes forward-looking statements as defined by the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, which are subject to risks and uncertainties outlined in the company's SEC filings. The information is current as of the release date, and Lotus Tech is not obligated to update these statements unless legally required.

#### Financial Overview

For the nine months ended September 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported significant financial changes compared to the same period in 2023:

- **Total Revenues:** Increased to $652,823,000 in 2024 from $317,941,000 in 2023.
  - **Sales Revenues:** Rose to $624,249,000 (2024) from $305,480,000 (2023).
  - **Service Revenues:** Increased to $28,574,000 (2024) from $12,461,000 (2023).
  
- **Total Costs of Revenues:** Increased to $593,708,000 in 2024 from $284,193,000 in 2023.
  - **Cost of Goods Sold:** Rose to $580,820,000 (2024) from $275,246,000 (2023).
  - **Cost of Services:** Increased to $12,888,000 (2024) from $8,947,000 (2023).
  
- **Gross Profit:** Increased to $59,115,000 in 2024 from $33,748,000 in 2023.

- **Total Operating Expenses:** Increased to $656,860,000 in 2024 from $541,624,000 in 2023.
  - **R&D Expenses:** Slightly decreased to $227,525,000 (2024) from $234,547,000 (2023).
  - **Selling and Marketing Expenses:** Increased to $259,804,000 (2024) from $191,231,000 (2023).
  - **General and Administrative Expenses:** Increased to $175,342,000 (2024) from $118,203,000 (2023).

- **Operating Loss:** Increased to $597,745,000 in 2024 from $507,876,000 in 2023.
- **Net Loss:** Increased to $666,520,000 in 2024 from $526,524,000 in 2023.
  - **Net Loss Attributable to Ordinary Shareholders:** Increased to $665,118,000 (2024) from $518,873,000 (2023).
  - **Loss per Ordinary Share:** $1.05 in 2024 compared to $1.10 in 2023.
  
- **Weighted Average Shares Outstanding:** Increased to 636,737,124 in 2024 from 474,621,603 in 2023.

#### Comprehensive Loss

- **Total Comprehensive Loss:** Reported at $(666,172,000) for 2024 compared to $(503,032,000) in 2023.
- **Total Comprehensive Loss Attributable to Ordinary Shareholders:** $(664,770,000) in 2024 versus $(495,445,000) in 2023.

#### Quarterly Performance (Three Months Ended September 30, 2024)

- **Total Revenues:** Increased to $254,708,000 in 2024 from $187,906,000 in 2023.
- **Net Loss:** Increased to $206,268,000 in 2024 from $173,582,000 in 2023.
- **Loss per Ordinary Share:** $0.30 in 2024 compared to $0.37 in 2023.
- **Total Comprehensive Loss:** $205,931,000 for 2024 compared to $183,094,000 in 2023.

#### Assets and Liabilities Overview (in thousands)

- **Total Assets:** Increased from $1,584,678 (Dec 31, 2023) to $2,587,224 (Sept 30, 2024).
  - **Current Assets:** Rose from $883,712 to $1,293,960, with cash decreasing from $418,941 to $191,300, while restricted cash increased significantly from $7,873 to $465,064.
  - **Non-Current Assets:** Increased from $700,966 to $1,293,264, including new loans receivable from related parties of $280,808.

- **Total Liabilities:** Rose from $2,411,850 (Dec 31, 2023) to $2,964,797 (Sept 30, 2024).
  - **Current Liabilities:** Increased from $1,757,281 to $2,129,955, with short-term borrowings from third parties rising from $226,772 to $703,130.
  - **Non-Current Liabilities:** Increased from $654,569 to $834,842, including new convertible notes due to related parties of $113,701.

#### Mezzanine Equity and Shareholders' Deficit (in thousands)

- **Series Pre-A and Series A Redeemable Convertible Preferred Shares:** Valued at $184,509 and $199,021 respectively as of Dec 31, 2023, with no corresponding values for Sept 30, 2024.
- **Total Shareholders' Deficit:** Increased from $(1,210,702) to $(377,573), with the accumulated deficit rising from $(1,588,773) to $(2,253,891).
- **Total Liabilities, Mezzanine Equity, and Shareholders' Deficit:** Totaled $2,587,224 as of Sept 30, 2024, compared to $1,584,678 at the end of 2023.

#### Non-GAAP Results

- **Adjusted Net Loss (Nine Months 2024):** $632,955,000.
- **Adjusted EBITDA (Nine Months 2024):** $(563,366,000).

This summary encapsulates the key financial metrics and results for Lotus Technology Inc. for the nine months and three months ended September 30, 2024, highlighting significant increases in revenues and losses, alongside a detailed breakdown of expenses and comprehensive losses. metadata.title_summary:
title: Powers of Attorney 
summary: The "Powers of Attorney" chapter outlines the legal framework governing the authority granted by nominee equity holders of Variable Interest Entities (VIEs) to directors of Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprises (WFOEs) or designated individuals. These Powers of Attorney are executed unconditionally and irrevocably, empowering the attorney-in-fact to act on behalf of the nominee equity holders in all matters related to their equity interests.

Key rights conferred upon the attorney-in-fact include the ability to attend VIE shareholders’ meetings, sign resolutions, exercise voting rights, and manage the sale, transfer, or pledge of equity interests. Furthermore, the attorney-in-fact is authorized to appoint senior management for the VIE, sign necessary documents, and file essential paperwork with Chinese regulatory authorities. This authority extends to rights associated with VIE bankruptcy, ensuring comprehensive control over the equity holder's interests.

The Powers of Attorney remain valid until the nominee equity holders cease to hold their positions or until the WFOE formally terminates these powers. This structure is crucial for maintaining operational control and compliance within the regulatory framework governing foreign investments in China, particularly in sectors where VIEs are utilized to navigate restrictions on foreign ownership.

On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Corporation, through its subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at the consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation purchased a one-year convertible note (the “Note”) with the principal of US$10.0 million issued by Ecarx. Upon the listing of Ecarx on Nasdaq on December 21, 2022, the Note was automatically converted into the Class A ordinary shares of Ecarx (the “listed equity securities”) at a conversion price of US$9.5 per share. As of June 30, 2023 and December 31, 2022, the fair value of the listed equity securities was US$7.3 million and US$8.4 million, respectively. In September 2021, Lotus Technology's wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Ningbo Juhe Yinqing Enterprise Management Consulting Partnership (Limited Partnership) provided guarantees and other features to the investor, which were accounted for as shareholder contributions at their estimated fair value at the respective issuance date of each tranche of loans.

title: Other Related Party Transactions 
summary: Lotus Tech is actively involved in the sale of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), auto parts, and offers research and development (R&D) and consulting services to related parties. In the first half of 2023, the company saw a significant increase in service revenue from related parties, reaching $5.0 million compared to $1.9 million in the same period of 2022. Product sales also grew, with $807,000 in BEVs and auto parts sold to related parties in early 2023, a notable rise from no sales in early 2022. Accounts receivable from related parties increased to $13.6 million by June 30, 2023, up from $8.5 million at the end of 2022.

Lotus Tech's financial activities include substantial R&D expenses, totaling $40.3 million in the first half of 2023, primarily for services from Geely Holding's subsidiaries. The company has also engaged in significant purchases from related parties, with total purchases reaching $264.4 million in early 2023, a sharp increase from $10.1 million in the same period of 2022. This includes a notable rise in inventories, which were valued at $122.7 million as of June 30, 2023.

In terms of financial agreements, Lotus Tech has signed various contracts with related parties, including a license agreement with Zhejiang Liankong for electric automotive technology, initially valued at approximately $888.2 million but later reduced to around $306.3 million. The company also entered into a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, granting exclusive global distribution rights for certain vehicle models for approximately $22.3 million.

Lotus Tech's financial obligations to related parties include accrued expenses and accounts payable, which have increased significantly, reflecting the company's reliance on related parties for operational support. The company has also engaged in leasing arrangements, with recognized right-of-use assets and lease liabilities reported over various periods.

Overall, Lotus Tech's financial activities underscore a robust interdependence with related parties, particularly for R&D and operational needs, as evidenced by the substantial amounts recorded in service revenues, purchases, and liabilities. The company's strategic agreements and financial transactions highlight its ongoing efforts to strengthen its market position and operational capabilities in the electric vehicle sector.

title: Spousal Consent Letters 
summary: The spouses of nominee equity holders have executed Spousal Consent Letters, affirming their unconditional agreement to the disposal of equity interests in the VIE held by their spouses, as detailed in several agreements, including the Equity Pledge Agreement and Exclusive Purchase Option Agreement. They acknowledge that they hold no rights to these equity interests and will not assert any claims in the future. all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 27, 2023, following discussions between Lotus Technology Corporation with Geely and Etika, respectively, regarding the proposed call option arrangement for the Capitalization Plan, representatives of Lotus Technology Corporation proposed to representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp that, rather than granting Lotus Technology Corporation a call option, each of Geely and Etika be granted a put option to sell their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technology Sdn Bhd (or such other entity holding the equity interests in LGIL at the time when the call options are exercised) to Lotus Technology Corporation. The exercise of the put option would be subject to the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by the LGIL group in 2024 exceeds 5,000. Following additional communications that same day, L Catterton Acquisition Corp agreed to the change from the call option arrangement to the put option arrangement. On the same day, representatives of Skadden circulated drafts of the Put Option Agreements to representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp and Kirkland & Ellis, reflecting the foregoing change in option arrangement. Subsequently and up until the execution of the Put Option Agreements on January 31, 2023, some final comments were exchanged between representatives of Skadden and Kirkland & Ellis. Also on January 27, 2023, Mr. Feng and Mr. Lee from Lotus Technology Corporation and Mr. Bhagat and Mr. Jin from L Catterton Acquisition Corp discussed the next steps for the Proposed Business Combination and set January 31, 2023 as the target signing date.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and associated Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA represent the culmination of extensive negotiations between the two parties, including their representatives. This summary provides an overview of LCAA's establishment, its prior business combination efforts, and the evaluations and negotiations surrounding LTC. LCAA, a blank check company founded in the Cayman Islands by affiliates of L Catterton on January 5, 2021, aims to facilitate mergers and similar business combinations. Following its IPO on March 15, 2021, which raised approximately $294.7 million, LCAA began actively seeking potential merger targets, focusing on companies with strong fundamentals and growth potential.

Over the course of its search, LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, engaging in non-disclosure agreements with 24 of them, but ultimately did not pursue any opportunities until discussions with LTC began. Initial interactions between LCAA and LTC commenced in early 2022, leading to a series of meetings and due diligence efforts. Throughout this period, LCAA representatives and LTC engaged in discussions regarding valuation, with LTC proposing an enterprise value of $12 billion, while LCAA indicated a maximum of $10 billion.

By September 2022, LCAA and LTC had drafted a non-binding Letter of Intent (LOI) proposing an enterprise value between $10 billion and $12 billion, contingent upon a merger with LGIL. Following further negotiations, the LOI was finalized on November 9, 2022, and due diligence commenced, focusing on various aspects of LTC's business.

The negotiations continued into early 2023, culminating in the execution of the Original Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents on January 31, 2023. The agreement outlined the terms of the merger, including a pre-money equity valuation of $5.5 billion to $6 billion, and established conditions for additional financing and collaboration post-merger. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made to refine terms related to share distribution and sponsor arrangements.

As of October 11, 2023, the parties signed the First Amended and Restated Agreement and Plan of Merger, which modified the structure of share distribution for LCAA Public Shareholders. Further amendments to the Sponsor Support Agreement were made on November 13, 2023, adjusting the vesting conditions of Sponsor Shares and outlining conditions for future business collaborations. Overall, the merger aims to enhance operational efficiencies and market reach, positioning the combined entity for growth in the competitive landscape.
